user_query:
I’m getting ready for a coordination call with our UN counterparts, and I need your help debugging our small aggregation script and preparing communication materials.

What I need you to do in this workspace:
1) Debug and fix the provided Python script so that the mentor-hours summary is calculated correctly.
- Inputs: input/engagements.csv and scripts/aggregate_partners.py
- Required output: outputs/summary.csv
- Acceptance: After your fix, running the aggregator should produce outputs/summary.csv that matches tests/expected_summary.csv exactly in values and rows (you can use tests/test_aggregate.py to verify).

Known symptoms with the current script (for context): it appears to undercount mentors when roles are capitalized differently, and the hours are being combined incorrectly.

2) Perform search engine queries from the Internet to confirm we’re using the correct country codes for two records in the input data, and capture this in meeting notes.
- Please search for and consult official standards sources (e.g., United Nations Statistics Division resources or ISO standards) to confirm the ISO alpha-3 codes for DR Congo and Tanzania (the codes in our data are COD and TZA).
- Do not include direct URLs. Instead, record the document or dataset titles and the publisher/organization (e.g., United Nations Statistics Division or ISO), along with the year if available.
- Deliverable: outputs/meeting_notes.md containing:
  • A short "Debugging summary" explaining the root cause(s) you found and the fix at a high level.
  • A "Search log" listing at least three search queries you used, each with the search engine name and rationale for the query.
  • A "Standards confirmation" section that states the codes you verified (COD and TZA), the corresponding country names you confirmed, and the authoritative sources you consulted (title + publisher/organization + year if available, no direct URLs).
  • An "Action items" section with 3–5 concrete next steps for our undergrad (e.g., data validation checks, code comments to add, or test coverage improvements).

3) Draft an email to our UN data liaison to accompany the corrected summary.
- Deliverable: outputs/email_draft.txt that:
  • Is addressed to un.data.liaison@example.org with a concise subject line reflecting the mentor hours summary and data validation.
  • Summarizes the bug, the correction made, and confirms adherence to UN/ISO country code standards based on your web confirmation.
  • Proposes two alternative 30-minute time windows for a brief follow-up (use generic placeholders, no scheduling links) and lists 2–3 agenda bullets.

Notes and constraints:
- Do not rely on any private systems or credentials. Use only the local files in this workspace and publicly available information via search.
- Keep outputs restricted to the specified paths:
  • outputs/summary.csv
  • outputs/meeting_notes.md
  • outputs/email_draft.txt
- Do not add new files outside of outputs/ unless they are necessary for the code fix.
- Please ensure tests/test_aggregate.py passes after your fix and that outputs/summary.csv content aligns with tests/expected_summary.csv.

Thank you—this will help our undergrad see a clean debugging workflow and give us a tidy package for our UN partners. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
